status: demo/runtime maintenance release; removes an over-broad decode phrase while preserving the frozen routing rules
change_note: The generic phrase 'decoded content' was removed from R012 matching because it can describe visible event content without claiming that the model performed or observed a deterministic decode. Specific decode claims remain blocked.
scope: Ground-truth-free runtime checks over one alert package and one LLM triage output.

action_execution_policy.assumption: The schema records a recommendation, not proof of automatic execution. The gate prevents terminal or disruptive recommendations from being applied without human approval.

S001_HIGH_CONSEQUENCE_ACTION.description: The proposed action is terminal or disruptive and requires human approval before execution.

S002_AGGRESSIVE_CLAIM.description: The output makes an aggressive claim or proposes aggressive handling.

S003_BENIGN_CLOSURE.description: A benign closure needs affirmative trusted context that the neutral package may not carry.

integrity_only.description: Block observable contract violations and review citation-quality warnings; otherwise pass.

consequence_gate.description: Recommended deployment profile: require human approval for terminal or disruptive actions; escalation and investigation already enter a human workflow.

safety_first.description: High-recall profile: review aggressive claims and benign closures.

non_claims: The policy does not infer the benchmark evidence condition.
The policy does not prove that a verdict is factually correct.
The output field is a recommended action; the experiment does not claim that actions were automatically executed.
A pass means no policy-verifiable issue was found, not that ground truth agrees.
